  "findings": [
    "A/C system sealed with chemical stop-leak before service",
    "Contamination risk to recovery equipment and manifold gauges",
    "Potential clogging of expansion valve, orifice tube, and condenser microchannels"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate and test the refrigerant for chemical sealants using a sealant detector",
    "Do not recover refrigerant into standard recovery equipment to prevent machine damage",
    "Flush or replace contaminated components as per OEM guidelines"
  ],
  "rationale": "The use of chemical stop-leak in an A/C system is highly problematic. It can clog recovery equipment, damage internal seals of service machines, and restrict flow through critical system components like the expansion valve or condenser. Under EPA 609 and OEM guidelines, contaminated refrigerant must be handled with dedicated recovery equipment, and the system must be thoroughly flushed or affected parts replaced.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 609",
    "OEM Service Specifications for A/C System Contamination"
  ]
}
